EU AI Act · GDPR · AI Security

Your company is using AI. Do you know what that legally means?

The EU AI Act is in force. Axionly helps organisations understand their AI systems, their legal obligations, and their security risks — before a regulator does.

Book a Free Discovery Call See what an assessment covers
AI Risk Assessment 5 business days
EU AI Act classification Mapped
GDPR data processing Reviewed
Prompt injection risk 2 findings
Vendor data exposure 1 finding
Internal AI policy Assessed
Prioritised action plan included 28 pages
Regulatory update

The EU AI Act's high-risk provisions are already enforceable. Organisations using AI in HR, credit, healthcare, or customer-facing systems may already be in breach. Does your organisation know which category it falls into?

The problem

Most companies are using AI.
Very few are managing it.

  • No inventory

    Most organisations do not have a complete list of the AI tools in use across their teams. Shadow AI is widespread — tools adopted without IT or legal review.

  • No compliance

    The EU AI Act creates specific legal obligations depending on the type of AI system in use. Most organisations have not assessed their exposure or identified which systems are in scope.

  • No security

    AI systems introduce new attack surfaces: prompt injection, data leakage through third-party APIs, and uncontrolled model access. Standard IT security reviews do not cover these.

The solution

Axionly gives you the full picture.

We are an AI governance and security consultancy. We assess your AI systems against your legal obligations under the EU AI Act and GDPR, surface your real security risks, and hand you a clear, prioritised action plan.

Fixed scope. Fixed price. Delivered in five business days.

See our services

  • Legal clarity

    Know exactly which of your AI systems fall under the EU AI Act — and what you must do about each one.

  • Security depth

    AI-specific vulnerabilities assessed by a team with a cybersecurity background — not just governance theory.

  • Actionable output

    No abstract reports. Every finding is tied to a specific owner, a concrete action, and a recommended deadline.

Assessment aligned with

  • EU AI Act Reg. 2024/1689
  • GDPR Compliant practice
  • ISO 42001 Aligned

Trusted by organisations in Romania and across the European Union

How it works

Five days. One clear answer.

  1. Scoping Call

    01

    Day 0 · Free

    A 20-minute conversation to confirm scope and whether an assessment is the right fit.

  2. Review

    02

    Day 1

    We analyse your AI systems, vendor contracts, and any existing governance policies.

  3. Assessment

    03

    Days 2–4

    We map your legal obligations and security posture — independently, with minimal time from your team.

  4. Report & Briefing

    04

    Day 5

    Full written report delivered, followed by a 30-minute executive walkthrough call.

Learn more about the process →

Book a free 20-minute discovery call. No commitment, no jargon. We will tell you honestly whether an assessment makes sense for your organisation.
